heading: Engine cut-off switches
label: 4312 Engine cut-off switches

children.heading: .—Installation Requirement
children.label: a .—Installation Requirement
children.text: A manufacturer, distributor, or dealer that installs propulsion machinery and associated starting controls on a covered recreational vessel shall equip such vessel with an engine cut-off switch and engine cut-off switch link that meet American Boat and Yacht Council Standard A–33, as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Frank LoBiondo Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2018 ().Public Law 115–282

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: An individual operating a covered recreational vessel shall use an engine cut-off switch link while operating on plane or above displacement speed.

children.label: A
children.text: the main helm of the covered vessel is installed within an enclosed cabin; or

children.label: B
children.text: the vessel does not have an engine cut-off switch and is not required to have one under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Education on Cut-off Switches
children.label: c .—Education on Cut-off Switches
children.text: The Commandant of the Coast Guard, through the National Boating Safety Advisory Committee established under section 15105, may initiate a boating safety program on the use and benefits of cut-off switches for recreational vessels.

children.heading: Availability of Standard for Inspection.—
children.label: d Availability of Standard for Inspection.—
A
children.text: the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Coast Guard Office of Design and Engineering Standards; and

children.label: D
children.text: the National Archives and Records Administration.

children.heading: .—Availability
children.label: 2 .—Availability
children.text: The standard submitted under paragraph (1) shall be kept on file and available for public inspection at such Coast Guard office and the National Archives and Records Administration.

children.label: A
children.text: less than 26 feet overall in length; and

children.label: B
children.text: capable of developing 115 pounds or more of static thrust.

children.heading: .—Dealer
children.label: 2 .—Dealer
children.text: The term “dealer” means any person who is engaged in the sale and distribution of recreational vessels or associated equipment to purchasers whom the seller in good faith believes to be purchasing any such vessel or associated equipment for purposes other than resale.

children.heading: .—Distributor
children.label: 3 .—Distributor
children.text: The term “distributor” means any person engaged in the sale and distribution of recreational vessels and associated equipment for the purposes of resale.

children.heading: .—Manufacturer
children.label: 4 .—Manufacturer
children.text: The term “equipment manufacturer” means any person engaged in the manufacture, construction, or assembly of recreational vessels or associated equipment, or the importation of recreational vessels into the United States for subsequent sale.

children.heading: .—Propulsion machinery
children.label: 5 .—Propulsion machinery
children.text: The term “propulsion machinery” means a self-contained propulsion system, and includes, but is not limited to, inboard engines, outboard motors, and sterndrive engines.

children.heading: .—Static thrust
children.label: 6 .—Static thrust
children.text: The term “static thrust” means the forward or backwards thrust developed by propulsion machinery while stationary.
